text:
```yaml infra-staging-egreen/workspace/ftp.yaml
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: sftpgo-config
data:
  SFTPGO_FTPD__BINDINGS__0__PORT: "2121"
  SFTPGO_FTPD__PASSIVE_PORT_RANGE__START: "30000"
  SFTPGO_FTPD__PASSIVE_PORT_RANGE__END: "30005"
  SFTPGO_DATA_PROVIDER__DRIVER: "postgresql"
  SFTPGO_DATA_PROVIDER__NAME: "sftpgo"
  SFTPGO_DATA_PROVIDER__HOST: "db-sftpgo"
  SFTPGO_DATA_PROVIDER__USERNAME: "sftpgo"
  SFTPGO_DATA_PROVIDER__PASSWORD: "sftpgociw8sWECsfgdd29fH2nwhkB"
  SFTPGO_DATA_PROVIDER__POOL_SIZE: "10"
  SFTPGO_HTTPD__BRANDING__NAME: "eGreen FTP server"
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Secret
metadata:
  name: sftpgo-secret
type: Opaque
data:
  SFTPGO_DATA_PROVIDER__PASSWORD: c2Z0cGdvY2l3OHNXRUNzZmdkZDI5Zkgybndoa0I= # Base64-encoded value
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: sftpgo
spec:
  type: LoadBalancer
  ports:
    - port: 21
      targetPort: 2121
      protocol: TCP
  selector:
    app: sftpgo
---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: sftpgo
spec:
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: sftpgo
  replicas: 1
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: sftpgo
    spec:
      containers:
        - name: sftpgo
          image: drakkan/sftpgo:latest
          ports:
            - containerPort: 2121
            - containerPort: 8080
              name: web-ui
          envFrom:
            - configMapRef:
                name: sftpgo-config
            - secretRef:
                name: sftpgo-secret
          volumeMounts:
            - name: sftpgo-data-volume
              mountPath: /srv/sftpgo/data
              # subPath: sftpgo-data
            - name: certs-volume
              mountPath: /srv/sftpgo/certs
              readOnly: true
      volumes:
        - name: certs-volume
          secret:
            secretName: sftpgo-web-ui-tls
        - name: sftpgo-data-volume
          persistentVolumeClaim:
            claimName: sftpgo-data-pvc

---
# Important to let the volume claim outside of the deployment to prevent delete it in the case of deployment deletion
# As it will store the ftp data we need to keep it
apiVersion: v1
kind: PersistentVolumeClaim
metadata:
  name: sftpgo-data-pvc
spec:
  accessModes:
    - ReadWriteOnce
  resources:
    requests:
      storage: 1Gi
  storageClassName: sbs-default-retain
---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: StatefulSet
metadata:
  name: db-sftpgo
spec:
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: db-sftpgo
  serviceName: "db-sftpgo"
  replicas: 1
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: db-sftpgo
    spec:
      containers:
        - name: postgres-sftpgo
          image: postgres:16
          ports:
            - containerPort: 5432
          env:
            - name: POSTGRES_DB
              value: sftpgo
            - name: POSTGRES_USER
              value: sftpgo
            - name: POSTGRES_PASSWORD
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: sftpgo-secret
                  key: SFTPGO_DATA_PROVIDER__PASSWORD
          volumeMounts:
            - name: postgres-data
              mountPath: /var/lib/postgresql/data
              subPath: postgres-data
  volumeClaimTemplates:
    - metadata:
        name: postgres-data
      spec:
        accessModes: [ "ReadWriteOnce" ]
        resources:
          requests:
            storage: 10Gi
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: db-sftpgo
spec:
  type: ClusterIP
  ports:
    - port: 5432
      targetPort: 5432
      protocol: TCP
  selector:
    app: db-sftpgo
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: sftpgo-web-ui
spec:
  type: LoadBalancer
  ports:
    - port: 8080
      targetPort: 8080
      protocol: TCP
      name: web-ui
  selector:
    app: sftpgo
---
apiVersion: traefik.io/v1alpha1
kind: IngressRoute
metadata:
  name: sftpgo-web-ui
  namespace: egreen
spec:
  entryPoints:
    - web
  routes:
    - match: Host(`ftp.k8-test.egreen.fr`)
      kind: Rule
      middlewares:
        - name: redirect-https
      services:
        - name: sftpgo-web-ui
          port: 8080
---
apiVersion: traefik.io/v1alpha1
kind: IngressRoute
metadata:
  name: sftpgo-web-ui-tls
  namespace: egreen
spec:
  entryPoints:
    - websecure
  routes:
    - match: Host(`ftp-ui.k8-test.egreen.fr`)
      kind: Rule
      services:
        - name: sftpgo-web-ui
          port: 8080
  tls:
    secretName: sftpgo-web-ui-tls

---
apiVersion: cert-manager.io/v1
kind: Certificate
metadata:
  name: ftp-ui.k8-test.egreen.fr
spec:
  dnsNames:
    - ftp-ui.k8-test.egreen.fr
    - ftp.k8-test.egreen.fr
  secretName: sftpgo-web-ui-tls
  issuerRef:
    name: letsencrypt-prod # Use the issuer created previously
    kind: ClusterIssuer
```

Add port for passive ftp in range 30000-30005 in sftpgo service
To add the passive FTP port range (30000-30005) to the `sftpgo` service, you need to include additional port mappings in the `Service` definition. Here's the updated YAML with the required changes:

```yaml
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: sftpgo
spec:
  type: LoadBalancer
  ports:
    - port: 21
      targetPort: 2121
      protocol: TCP
    - port: 30000
      targetPort: 30000
      protocol: TCP
    - port: 30001
      targetPort: 30001
      protocol: TCP
    - port: 30002
      targetPort: 30002
      protocol: TCP
    - port: 30003
      targetPort: 30003
      protocol: TCP
    - port: 30004
      targetPort: 30004
      protocol: TCP
    - port: 30005
      targetPort: 30005
      protocol: TCP
  selector:
    app: sftpgo
```

### Explanation of Changes:
1. **Added Ports for Passive FTP**:
   - Each port in the range `30000-30005` is explicitly defined in the `ports` section of the `Service`.
   - Each port maps the `port` (external) to the `targetPort` (internal) for the container.

2. **Protocol**:
   - The protocol for all ports is set to `TCP`, as FTP operates over TCP.

### Why This Is Necessary:
Passive FTP requires the server to open a range of ports for data transfer. These ports must be exposed in the Kubernetes `Service` to allow external clients to connect to the server.

After applying this updated configuration, the `sftpgo` service will expose the passive FTP port range (30000-30005) in addition to the control port (21).
